item.title: Nope, sorry
item.content:encoded: I won't downvote it because I normally only do that for outright bad/sloppy writing, but this definitely isn't intriguing me enough to upvote. Sorry.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: With no indication the image has been released under Creative Commons, it has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always been my favourite.

item.title: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- Hide

To link to another skip featuring light courier, I propose the following edit:

The only other place we can go is the maincontinent, where the Light Courier company controls all the people.

to

The only other place we can go is the maincontinent, where the Light Courier company controls all the people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trivia. SCP-878-TH using the same image ;) (the article is completely different though).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thoight "Ohhh, special explanation for ghosts". Then it turned into so much more.

+1 for you, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rather like this. It's quite creepy without being actively dangerous: while there are so many Skips that are based around our world having something horribly wrong happen, this one's a window into someone else's world being screwed up - and their failed attempt to escape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm partial to this; it makes for an interesting concept, but I'm not sure about the portal to another dimension sort of thing, but that's more of a personal taste. Either way, it's well-written and the image adds to the odd feeling it gives off. It has me interested, which is what's important. I'm upvoting on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad someone finally used that weird-ass picture, and used it with an equally weird concept. A +1 to you sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure if I just posted this at a bad time, or if everyone else is neutral. If the latter is the problem, I may look into reworking this.

Edit: Yeah, I won't take it down. I like it the way it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remember, tags aren't in-universe. They're a way of navigating the wiki, and in order for this to be extradimensional, I feel like there should be slightly more explicit mention of why we should believe that. It's entirely up to you, though, which is why I didn't just go in there and change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP ranks high enough on the WTF meter to make me upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I left those questions unanswered for a reason, mainly because I don't want to. I haven't seen an article that's made me wonder for a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I got the idea from a dream, and remembered seeing the image on VR. I'm glad that I got that you got that sort of feeling from it, that's what I was going for. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a collection of ghost statues. They follow the movement of the earth, but matter moves through them without interaction. They're made of many kinds of (incorporeal) matter. They escaped from a global disaster in another universe. The same disaster may be in early stages here. And they're Russian.

It's nicely written, and atmospheric, but there's no implied larger canvas than the one given. Why are they statues? Why are they intangible? Why do they follow the earth? There's no answers, just mystery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's very atmospheric overall, which isn't something you get often with with skips, but I can't shake the feeling this was written off the image. And while the image is neat, overall, it's just not that interesting.

Also, GTrans choked on "узрейте". halp plz

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're Russian refugees from elsewhere, except they're coming out a little weird. That's all I'll say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with the sentiment that I don't really get this. It's kinda cool, but I don't really grasp the overall concept, so I can't upvote, though the writing does avoid the downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading the final version, the rearranging of addenda makes it clearer that the -A was some kind of portal through which they were escaping, which means now the portal is gone and the rest of them is trapped in the tyrannous shattered world (I completely missed this when reading the draft), and it's all the Foundation's fault. All of that together is enough for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't think I get this one. It's not badly written, but the concept is just…there, I suppose. It's not grabbing me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess that's more of a headcanon thing… Maybe I should leave it up to the reader to decide that, and I guess the Foundation wouldn't assume it is, if they tagged. I think I'll revert it. Now I'm making too many edits. :c

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there any particular reason you added extradimensional, too? I may be misreading, but I see no evidence to suggest why it would be considered an extradimensional entity.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to anqxyr, Azzleflux, Eskobar, and chubert for reviewing this.

Image source is here: http://www.antonygormley.com/sculpture/item-view/id/225#p6

+ Small Spoiler
- Hide

These people came from the same universe as Light Courier Enterprises.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rather than "palpable", it should probably be "tangible".
